name: Juwan Johnson
headline: Volume's there for Johnson, but the touchdowns won't follow
body:
Johnson finally hit a true featured role in 2025, ranking third among tight ends with 102 targets, an 18.1% target share, and a 0.384 WOPR. He converted that into 77 grabs for 889 yards on a tidy 75.5% catch rate, and his efficiency backs the workload up: 0.345 EPA per play, a 55.9% success rate, and 1.7 yards per route run across 522 routes. The volume is real and earned.

The problem is the scoring. Just three receiving touchdowns and only eight red-zone looks capped him at 102.9 fantasy points despite all that usage, leaving him a TE2. Now New Orleans has imported pass-catching tight ends to crowd the room, and at 28 turning 29 the three-year climb is likelier to flatten than extend. Projections near 67 catches and 750 yards keep him a steady streamer, drafted late as a back-end starter rather than a difference-maker.
